name: Professor Aronnax
description: Professor Aronnax, the intrepid marine biologist, is depicted as a man in his late forties, bearing the weathered wisdom of countless expeditions. His face, framed by a neatly trimmed, silvering beard and a receding hairline revealing a high forehead, is etched with fine lines around his keen, grey-blue eyes – eyes that constantly observe and analyze. A prominent, slightly hooked nose gives him an air of scholarly intensity. He typically wears a practical, yet refined ensemble: a meticulously tailored, olive-green tweed suit, often speckled with dried seawater stains. A high-collared, cream-colored linen shirt peeks from beneath the jacket, accented by a loosely knotted, dark brown silk cravat. Over his suit, he often dons a long, canvas duster coat in muted ochre, its pockets overflowing with notebooks, pencils, and magnifying lenses. His hands, gnarled but nimble, are always ink-stained and often sport small nicks from handling botanical specimens. The overall color palette leans towards earthy tones – browns, greens, creams, and greys – reflecting his connection to the natural world and creating a visual harmony that echoes the delicate beauty he seeks to capture in his botanical illustrations.
